blocks.text: If the vehicle speed exceeds a predetermined value for a predetermined period of time when the accelerator pedal is depressed with contact 1 of the brake switch ON, or if deceleration/stopping is repeated a predetermined number of times after the vehicle speed exceeds a predetermined value with contact 1 of the brake switch OFF, the PGM-FI ECU determines that a failure has occurred. The PGM-FI ECU judges a failure and stores the DTC.
Detection frequency, detection sequence, detection time required, detection method type, OBD status

rows: *: ON sticking failure **: OFF sticking failure D/C: Drive Cycle
Ignition switch/ ENGINE START STOP switch
DTC requesting the prohibition of detection of the relevant DTC
Gas pedal To step on the pedal.

blocks.text: If any of the following is established

items.text: Cord between PGM-FI ECU and brake switch (BRK DIAG*) 1 (STIO SW)* 2 ) disconnection (including coupler disconnection or looseness)
Cord between PGM-FI ECU and brake switch (BRK DIAG*) 1 (STIO SW)* 2 ) power supply short circuit
Cord between PGM-FI ECU and brake switch (BRK DIAG*) 1 (STIO SW)* 2 ) ground fault of

blocks.text: *1: Vehicles equipped with the City Brake Active System
*2: Cars without City Brake Active System

If the vehicle is determined to have a fault, a temporary DTC is stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory. If the same failure is detected at the next D/C (two consecutive detections), the DTC and freeze data are stored without turning on the PGM-FI warning light.
Temporary DTCs, DTCs and freeze data are cleared by using the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or by removing the 12V battery terminal.
